dialogos.texto_en: Huh?\nAre Miku and Ichika over there?
Seems that way.
This mango pudding is so good...!
And this sweet potato tart too!\nIt's so fluffy!
We can even try some chinsuko biscuits.\nLet's give them a taste.
Ah, I just remembered something.\nThe person I co-starred with before\ntold me about the most delicious chinsuko in Okinawa.
Huh? Which shop?
I forgot where it was.\nAhaha, I should have taken notes.
But, can there really be that much difference in taste?
Well, that chinsuko is said to be exceptional.\nOnce you try it, you can't eat any other chinsuko.
That's something you can't forget.
Oooh, it's on the tip of my tongue!
What are you two doing?\nLooking for souvenirs?
Oh, Futaro-kun and Nino.
Yes, we're trying out some samples\nwhile looking for souvenirs.
Futaro and Nino, want to try some too?\nThis mango pudding is delicious.
Sure, let me have a bite.
It's really sweet.
But it has a natural sweetness.\nThe mango flavor is so rich.
On the other hand, there are some things that are\ndeliciously spicy, right?\nLike these Okinawa pepper rice crackers.
...Those are REALLY spicy.
But they smell and taste so good.
And the spiciness doesn't last.\nI'm thinking of buying some for the office.
Oh, if you want something sweet,\nthis black sugar chocolate is good.
Ah, well, this has a rich flavor,\nbut it's not too sweet.
Yes, it seems it'll match matcha soda.
I don't think so.
...It's really sweet.
Futaro-kun, don't you have any other thoughts\nbesides really 'sweet' or 'spicy'?\nIs just one word good enough for you?
Well, you know...
After all, it is Futaro. It can't be helped.
There you go again, spoiling him.
(Spoiling me...)
Souvenirs are good, but I should also buy something \nfor myself. Thus I won't have to worry about snacks for a while.
I should buy something too.\nHey, Futaro, what do you recommend?
Asking him is unreasonable...
(Gghhnn...\n This request is indeed a bit unreasonable!)
Why not go with a classic?\nIf it's selling well, it must be good.
(If it's a standard product, it should have a certain\n level of quality. This way, Nino won't be disappointed.\n Being a standard product is a strength in itself!)
I see.\nThen, let's go with mango pudding and chinsuko.
I'll go and pay for it now.\nThanks for your help, Futaro.
Okay.
How about choosing by the design of the \npackage? This one is quite elaborate,\nand even just looking at it is fun.
Oh, yes. This case could even be used\nfor holding small items.\nIt'll also remind you of Okinawa.
Which one...do you think is cutest, Futaro?
I don't know if it's cute,\nbut I think this one with the blue sky\nand ocean packaging is real nice.
Alright, then I'll go with that!
Huh? Is that really okay?
The things that you picked out for me are the best, Futaro.\nIt'll become a memory of mine just because you chose it.
I'll go and pay for it now.\nThanks for your help, Futaro.
Okay.
Seasonings might be good.
You're good at cooking, right?\nBottled seasonings using Okinawa peppers\nmight be more useful than rice crackers.
Yes, seasonings are a good idea.\nOkinawa peppers can be used as\na secret ingredient in various dishes.
Shikuwasa fruit vinegar is also drinkable%p-2;――
It might be fun to utilize it in a dressing or dessert.
O-ohh...\nIt's amazing how you keep coming up with ideas,\none after another, like that.
It's all thanks to you, Futaro.\nIf you hadn't given me this advice,\nI wouldn't have thought of buying seasonings.
I'll go and pay for it now.\nThanks for your help, Futaro.
Okay.
(Hmm, I wonder how well I performed.\n Leading the conversation is harder than I thought...)
Futaro, what's wrong?\nYou seem lost in thought.
Maybe Futaro-kun is trying his best in his own way.
He's finally starting to grasp\nthe difficulty of love.
Hang in there, Futaro.
